artifact: Lane-V verification record — ADR-056 (threeway scope-b sub-project 1: minimal operable mechanical-seat runtime)
subproject: threeway scope-b sub-project 1 — overseer_emit.py + bootstrap_emit.py + run_merge_gate.py(daemon hardening) + run_merge_gate.sh + E2E walking-skeleton

verifier.kind: independent Lane-V workflow — 5 cold-context dimension verifiers + 1 sequential mutation-execution agent + 3 adversarial refuters
verifier.model: sonnet (per standing directive; opus opt-in only)
verifier.trust: re-derived GO/NITS/FAIL from the actual diff + fresh runs + 18 executed mutations; did NOT trust the implementer report or commit messages; reviewer prompts were cold-context (no implementer findings cited — Rule #9)
verifier.non_authorship: verifier session did not author any of the commits under review (impl != verifier satisfied); all peers offline/stale => solo verify, Tier-A co-sign N/A

verdict_basis: no CRITICAL/MAJOR findings across all 5 dimensions; no scope violations; all 6 plan acceptance criteria backed by real, non-vacuous tests; every load-bearing safety/authority property proven load-bearing by a RED-as-expected mutation; the 3 stayed-green mutations were each adversarially refuted (high confidence) as non-defects.

evidence.full_threeway_suite: env -u GIT_INDEX_FILE .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/ -k threeway -q -> 376 passed, 1 skipped, 2803 deselected (118.20s)
evidence.ci_smoke: OK (R1/R3/R4/R5/R6 PASS; R2 WARN = pre-existing cv2 importorskip latent; one stale-SHA WARN at DECISIONS.md:2718 a38a3d69 = PRE-EXISTING in the ADR-054 block, NOT introduced by this slice — confirmed via git show e45f2cf6:DECISIONS.md)
evidence.check_no_ceremony: no ceremony detected (no NEW non-strict xfail / status-only assertion / invisible-green from this slice)
scope_match: zero changes to threeway/ library code or production pipeline (cinema/web_server) — all 14 changed files are new scripts + new tests + docs
no real refs/heads/main promotion code anywhere in the diff (grep clean)
no sub-project-2 seat<->bus wiring, no hardening-track, no automation-track creep

mutation_table.method: single sequential agent applied each recipe to the named source file, ran the named pin, recorded RED/GREEN, then restored via git checkout HEAD -- <file>; final git status == only the pre-existing seen/.migration cutover artifacts (tree_clean=true)

keystone_DD1.property: DD-1 (SAFETY-CRITICAL): run_merge_gate.py --main-ref default = refs/threeway/test-main, never refs/heads/main
keystone_DD1.result: RED-as-expected (ns.main_ref was refs/heads/main) -> the guard is load-bearing

red_as_expected_list: wrapper passes --main-ref refs/threeway/test-main (changed to refs/heads/main -> RED)
DD-2 wrapper exports NO PYTHONPATH (added export -> RED)
stop-at-top: _STOP checked before poll_once (moved after -> RED, poll_once ran)
overseer-key enforcement: brief signed with operator key -> dropped (RED)
non-overseer-key drop pin: removing the keystore-overwrite makes brief admitted -> RED (overwrite is the load-bearing step)
release_order subject_sha on ENVELOPE not payload (moved to payload -> RED)
bootstrap _append seat-key from ev.signer (forced overseer key -> candidate dropped -> RED)
two attestations have distinct ids (collision -> loop.py:116 assert fires -> RED)
bootstrap unresolvable-ref clean rc=1 (removed handler -> uncaught -> RED)
E2E release_order is mandatory (dropped emit -> ref did NOT advance, no merge_completed -> RED)

stayed_green_refuted_list.refutation: brief_version IS present in the payload (overseer_emit.py:61); reducer fallback never masks a live bug; the only gap is the test does not independently assert payload['brief_version'] -> optional test-strengthening NIT, not a defect (high confidence)

store.append always re-signs over the real seq, so pre-signing is redundant-not-harmful; the test is a round-trip authority pin (catches wrong key/payload/drop), not a sole-signing-step pin; DD-5 is enforced by code+comment (overseer_emit.py:32) -> non-defect (high confidence)

release_requested is a SIGNAL-ONLY daemon trigger; predicate.py never consults it; collect_candidate_ids does a raw scan and run_gate re-verifies authority (run_merge_gate.py:42-44); reducer intentionally omits it from _AUTHORIZED_SINGLETON_SEAT -> a forged one only wastes a gate run; missing signer-assert is an optional pin, not a security hole (high confidence)

nits: MINOR test_threeway_overseer_emit.py: add assert cg.payload['brief_version']==1 (cycle_go payload pin); add an AppendContentionExceeded rc=1 pin (DD-4 contention path untested)
NIT test_threeway_overseer_emit.py: only brief has an explicit wrong-key mutation pin; add one for release_order/re_verify_challenge for the envelope-split facts
MINOR test_threeway_bootstrap_emit.py: assert state.release_requested('A:c1').signer seat == 'coordinator' (one-liner makes the coordinator-key claim mutation-testable)
NIT test_threeway_bootstrap_emit.py: add an unclean-merge (conflicting branch) fixture for the shared ValueError handler
NIT test_threeway_merge_gate_daemon.py: the .sh wrapper is content-scanned but never subprocess-executed; add a run_merge_gate.sh --run-once smoke
NIT run_merge_gate.py:105-106: per-iteration except swallows infra errors with no structured persistent-failure signal (monitoring gap, not correctness)

MINOR/NIT test_threeway_e2e_walking_skeleton.py: redundant monkeypatch.setenv(THREEWAY_KEYSTORE) (add clarifying comment); pass --remote '' explicitly to sign_ci_result for symmetry; add an explicit assert that real refs/heads/main is unchanged
NIT (PRE-EXISTING, out-of-scope) DECISIONS.md:2718: stale-looking SHA a38a3d69 in the ADR-054 block is a subagent run-id, not a commit; ci_smoke false-positives it
NIT spec sys.path anchor: design spec says sign_ci_result.py:19-22; executable bootstrap is :20-22 (line 19 is a comment)
disposition: Sub-project 1 is operable + verified. NITS are non-blocking optional test-strengthening + one pre-existing out-of-scope doc nit; none require an xfail pin (no confirmed unfixed DEFECT — the 3 vacuous-pin candidates were refuted as non-defects). Next phase (hardening track / sub-project 2 / automation track) is a chief/user prioritization decision per the plan.
